root_cause: API circuit breaker permanently open (record_success() never called) triggered unbounded sub-agent spawn loop via work_cycle fallback path

timeline.note: 31-min silent gap — CB tripped, first cascade entry
FORENSIC: test_write.json probe written by sub-agent
FORENSIC: 3 parallel agents write same timestamp (divergent data)
agent_audit blocked by CB — system blind to its own scale
TRUE END: last disk write (01:49 log written at 01:52 per filesystem timestamp)
Expected cron tick — never fired. Silence.

forensic_findings: test_write.json (13 bytes) written at 01:22 UTC — sub-agent filesystem probe
Three parallel agents wrote 01:23 timestamp with divergent cycle numbers (89 / 123 / 127 sessions)
work_cycle_2026-02-28_0123.json written to disk at 01:49 UTC (26 min after nominal timestamp)
0129.json and 0134.json written 1 minute apart (17:35 and 17:36 PST) — parallel execution confirmed
Last disk write: 17:52 PST = 01:52 UTC. No files created after this.
31-minute silent gap: 23:17→23:48 UTC — CB had just tripped, no log produced

circuit_breaker.bug: record_success() never called; breaker stays permanently open

cascade_mechanics.spawn_pattern: work_cycle → api_client blocked → spawn sub-devsecops → sub-devsecops hits same CB → spawns another → repeat
cascade_mechanics.reason_prune_failed: All cascade sessions < 2h old — under the 24h prune threshold

fixes: CB-aware spawn gate: if CB open, log degraded and stop — do not spawn
Work cycle must accept 'degraded' as valid terminal state after 2 fallback attempts
Separate list_agents audit path from write-path circuit breaker
note_privacy: Network/IP fields stripped from all published logs. No API keys or auth tokens were present.
